summary: Safe implementation sequence for feature 006 starts by freezing shared contracts and tests, then extracts shared validation before building the authoring service, API endpoint, tool, registration, and final desktop/server routing verification. The critical blockers are validation extraction, basis-hash/stale-write primitives, phase-policy mutation gating, and authoring service contract stabilization before API/tool consumers are wired.

dependency_edges.reason: Contract fixtures and valid template factories should be available before extracting validation so parity assertions can be written against existing registry/API behavior.
Shared validation extraction must land before the field guide can assert required fields, allowed values, unsupported shapes, and issue-code repair hints without drifting.
Authoring service validation, conflict checks, and no-write mutation paths must call the shared validator rather than copying registry/API validation.
Basis hash helpers are part of the authoring service contract and must exist before update/delete stale-write behavior is finalized.
Create/update/duplicate/delete orchestration depends on stable basis-hash and validation result shapes.
The local authoring API route should wrap the service contract after service result envelopes, status mapping, and no-write behavior are stable.
Existing manual API endpoints should be migrated to shared validation before the new authoring endpoint is tested for parity.
Operation-level phase-policy helper must exist before tool input validation can deny mutating operations without blocking read-only operations.
Tool direct-service calls and result rendering depend on the finalized authoring service operation/result contract.
Tool desktop-server routing depends on the authoring API endpoint path and response envelope.
Global tool registration should happen only after tool metadata, schema, permissions, phase gating, and routing tests exist.
Desktop-server routing tests need the server endpoint available before verifying no direct-write fallback when CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is set.
Desktop-server routing is tool behavior and must be verified through the tool call path.
Runtime snapshot safety checks depend on mutating service operations existing so tests can assert they do not call or mutate workflow runtime/session state.
Final integration verification should run only after registration makes the new built-in tool visible in the assembled tool pools.
Final verification must include the desktop/server route and failure behavior before PR-readiness gates.
Final verification must prove active/historical workflow sessions remain untouched by authoring mutations.

parallel_batches.safety_reason: Initial contract/test fixture work is read-only or test-only and establishes stable expected behavior before production extraction.
Field guide alignment and phase-policy helper work touch separate service files after shared validation is available; neither should mutate persistence or route behavior.
API wrapper and tool implementation can proceed in parallel once the service contract is stable, but both must join before registration and desktop routing verification.
Desktop-server routing tests and runtime snapshot safety tests validate independent integration risks after API/tool/service surfaces exist.

join_points.validation_target: Baseline fixtures and existing behavior coverage for workflow template registry/API/tool patterns
join_points.pass_condition: Existing tests pass before production behavior is refactored; new fixture helpers do not change runtime behavior.
Registry and manual API validation still reject/normalize the same required fields, builtin conflicts, duplicate ids, unsupported shapes, and malformed configs.

join_points.validation_target: Guide drift and phase-policy mutation gating
join_points.pass_condition: Guide tests cover required groups/allowed values/unsupported shapes/repair hints, and policy tests prove read-only operations stay allowed while mutations are denied outside allowed phases.

join_points.validation_target: Authoring service contract, stale-write protocol, no-write failures, builtin copy, and persistence boundaries
join_points.pass_condition: All service operations return the required envelope; invalid, stale, builtin, malformed-config, missing-target, and conflict cases keep workflow config unchanged.

join_points.validation_target: Authoring API, tool metadata/schema/result rendering, and global registration
join_points.pass_condition: Authoring endpoint maps service results correctly; tool read-only/destructive/concurrency metadata is correct; registered tool appears in base/assembled pools only after tests pass.

join_points.validation_target: Desktop-server routing, no silent fallback, Settings refetch compatibility, and workflow session snapshot safety
join_points.pass_condition: When CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is set the tool posts to /api/workflows/templates/authoring, failed endpoint responses are no-write failures, successful server writes are visible via list/refetch, and runtime session state is not mutated.

join_points.validation_target: Full server/tool quality gate for implementation readiness
join_points.validation_command: bun run check:server && bun run verify
join_points.pass_condition: Server check and non-live PR gate pass, or blockers are recorded with latest artifact paths and failed lane logs.

serial_tasks.reason: Create/organize test fixtures and contract helper expectations first so later refactors can prove no behavior drift.
Extract shared workflow template validation before authoring service/API/tool work; this is the main foundational blocker.
Define authoring operation/result types and service skeleton after validation extraction so all consumers share one contract.
Implement canonical SHA-256 basis-hash helpers before update/delete mutation orchestration.
Implement create/update/duplicate/delete service behavior only after validation and stale-write primitives are stable.
Register the tool globally only after tool tests pass; premature registration risks exposing an unguarded mutating capability.
Run final focused and project gates after all joins; this is the readiness gate and should not be parallelized with source edits.

verification_gates.purpose: Prove baseline registry/API/policy behavior before validation extraction and policy changes.
Verify guide/list/inspect/validate and create-from-conversation support with shared validation and deterministic result envelopes.
Verify update, stale-write rejection, builtin copy-then-edit, unknown-field preservation, and no-write failure behavior.
Verify global tool availability with operation-level read-only/mutating/destructive metadata and active workflow phase-policy denial.
Verify local desktop/server authoring endpoint, desktop URL routing, no silent direct-write fallback, and Settings refetch compatibility.
Run the required server/tool quality gate and final non-live project PR verification.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Validation drift between registry, manual API, authoring API, and tool.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: JP2 requires shared validation parity tests before any authoring service or route is accepted.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Authoring service writes over malformed config, invalid drafts, stale manual edits, or builtin templates.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: JP4 requires no-write assertions for malformed config, invalid candidate, builtin mutation/delete, conflicts, missing targets, and stale basis hash.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Global registration exposes mutating authoring in forbidden workflow phases.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: JP3 and JP5 require operation-level phase-policy tests before registration.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Whole-tool phase filtering blocks safe guide/list/inspect/validate operations.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: Policy tests must prove read-only operations remain allowed even when mutation is denied.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Desktop-launched tool direct-writes and leaves desktop server registry cache stale.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: JP6 requires CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL tests proving POST /api/workflows/templates/authoring is used and direct-write fallback is not used on endpoint failure.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Tool registration happens before schema, metadata, routing, and permission tests.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: T010 is serial and depends on JP5 preconditions from tool/API tests.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Authoring mutations accidentally touch active or historical workflow session snapshots.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: JP6 requires service/runtime safety assertions that WorkflowRuntimeService/session state is not called or mutated by authoring operations.

risk_checkpoints.risk: Field guide becomes prompt text that drifts from validation rules.
risk_checkpoints.checkpoint: JP3 requires guide drift tests against required fields, allowed values, unsupported shapes, and repairable issue codes.

notes_for_leader: Suggested task IDs are provisional; preserve the dependency shape if renumbering.
Do not let API route work begin before the authoring service result envelope is stable.

Do not let tool registration happen before tool tests cover read-only, destructive, concurrency-safe=false, permission classifier text, phase denial, direct-service routing, and desktop-server routing.
Keep validation extraction as an early serial blocker; a tool-only validator violates the plan and creates the highest drift risk.
Treat desktop source edits as optional; if implementation touches desktop/src, add same-area Vitest/Testing Library coverage and run bun run check:desktop.

Final implementation handoff should include changed files, tests added, quality report path, coverage report path, pass/fail/skip counts, and explicit live-provider blocker if any live lane is unavailable.
